channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Pakistan and Canada Strengthen Bilateral Ties Through Diplomatic Engagement
item.description: Deputy Prime Minister Ishaq Dar emphasized Pakistan's commitment to strengthening bilateral relations with Canada during the visit of Canadian Foreign Minister Anita Anand. The delegation-level talks,...

item.title: Wayne Rooney criticises World Cup half-time show for being unengaging
item.description: The first World Cup final half-time show, which extended the traditional 15-minute break to 27 minutes, faced criticism from former England forward Wayne Rooney and fellow pundits. The event, curated ...

item.title: Wildfires threaten Manitoba's logging industry and economic stability
item.description: The Manitoba logging industry faces significant challenges as wildfires continue to burn through forests, threatening both the supply of timber and the livelihoods of workers. Arnold Reimer, a seasone...

item.title: Pulse Asia survey shows decline in Marcos' trust and approval ratings while Sara Duterte's ratings remain stable

item.title: Hamilton Not at Fault as Ferrari Penalized for Pitstop Incident
item.description: Lewis Hamilton was not held accountable after his car accidentally knocked over a Ferrari mechanic during a chaotic Belgian Grand Prix pitstop. The incident occurred when the mechanic stepped forward ...

item.title: Employers Face Legal Consequences for Non-Compliance with Pension Fund Contributions
item.description: A recent report reveals that 6,064 employers in South Africa are in arrears with retirement fund contributions, affecting approximately 590,000 employees. The total arrears amount to R8.33bn, with lat...

item.title: Firefighters battle blaze on Arthur’s Seat in Edinburgh
item.description: Firefighters are continuing their efforts to combat a wildfire that broke out on Arthur’s Seat in Edinburgh on Sunday evening. The Scottish Fire and Rescue Service (SFRS) first responded to the incide...

item.title: Palace Dismisses Cayetano's Claim About Marcos Rejecting P10,000 Cash Aid Proposal
item.description: The Palace Press Officer, Claire Castro, has publicly rebuked Senator Alan Peter Cayetano for his claim that President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. rejected a proposed P10,000 cash aid program for Filipino fa...

item.title: The UK's CPAC Conference: A Farcical Event Amid Political Unrest
item.description: The article details the inaugural CPAC Great Britain conference, a UK counterpart to the American alt-right political gathering. Held in East London's Greenwich, the event faced significant attendance...

item.title: Rohit Sharma's Record Century at Lord's Keeps India Alive in ODI Series Decider Against England

item.title: Call for Respect and Dignity for Security Guards Amid Exploitation Concerns
item.description: The article highlights the systemic mistreatment of security guards in South Africa, emphasizing their critical role in public safety while facing exploitation by employers and customers. It reference...

item.title: Conor McGregor Reveals Details of His Knee Injury and Recovery Plans
item.description: Conor McGregor, the Irish mixed martial arts icon, recently revealed the specifics of his right knee injury sustained during a UFC 329 fight against Max Holloway. The injury, which occurred in the fir...

item.title: FG launches N482m fund to support 12 Nigerian tech startups
item.description: The federal government has launched a $350,000 (about N482.4 million) investment fund under the iDICE Programme to support 12 tech-enabled Nigerian startups. The Bank of Industry (BoI) is implementing...

item.title: Smart City Projects Face Financial Strain as Funds Deplete
item.description: The article highlights the growing financial challenges faced by India's Smart Cities post the end of central funding in March 2025. With over 16 months since the cessation of funds, many cities like ...

item.title: England's Third-Place Finish at World Cup Sparks Reflection on Historic Achievement
item.description: England manager Thomas Tuchel expressed hope that his players would take pride in their third-place finish at the 2026 World Cup, marking their best performance since winning in 1966. The victory over...

item.title: Canadian FM Anita Anand's Official Visit to Pakistan Focuses on Trade and Bilateral Relations
item.description: Canadian Foreign Minister Anita Anand arrived in Islamabad on Monday for an official visit to Pakistan, marking her first trip to the country since assuming office. Deputy Prime Minister and Foreign M...

item.title: Greg Kroah-Hartman Advocates Rust for Linux's Future, Citing Bug Reduction and Developer Efficiency
item.description: Greg Kroah-Hartman, a key Linux kernel maintainer, has become a strong advocate for Rust, arguing that its ownership model and type system can significantly reduce common vulnerabilities in the Linux ...

item.title: Celebrating Jessica Patton Barkentin: A Glimpse into 1940s Fashion Photography
item.description: Jessica Patton Barkentin was a prominent fashion model of the mid-20th century, embodying the refined elegance and quiet sophistication of the 1940s and ’50s. Her career spanned collaborations with le...

item.title: Torres' Last-Minute Goal Secures Spain's World Championship Victory
item.description: The article details a pivotal moment in the World Cup final where Substitute Torres, playing for Spain, scored a critical goal in the dying minutes of the match. This dramatic last-minute goal turned ...
